summary: MCP module (1053-line index.ts) has zero dedicated test coverage
detail: No test file exists for packages/opencode/src/mcp/. Only 2 test files reference MCP — both use fully stubbed MCP services. Untested: connect/disconnect lifecycle, transport creation (stdio/SSE/StreamableHTTP), OAuth flow, tool list change notification handler, error handling, process cleanup, status transitions.

summary: MCP notification handler registered via setNotificationHandler never cleaned up on disconnect
detail: watch() at mcp/index.ts:531 registers client.setNotificationHandler(ToolListChangedNotificationSchema, ...). closeClient() at line 625 only calls client.close() — no handler removal. Handler closure captures State, client, bridge references. If SDK's close() doesn't clean up handlers, this is a memory leak.

summary: MCP registration failures silently swallowed in ACP/acp-next
detail: ACP agent.ts:1177 catches sdk.mcp.add() errors with log-only output. ACP-next service.ts:966 uses Effect.ignore — no logging, no alert to ACP client. A client requesting prompt execution with a broken MCP server would only see missing tools, not registration errors.

summary: 4 silent Effect.ignore calls on client.close() — state inconsistency risk
detail: mcp/index.ts uses Effect.tryPromise(() => client.close()).pipe(Effect.ignore) at lines 322, 498, 613, 629. If close() throws, the error is silently swallowed. s.clients[name] may still hold a dead client reference while s.status[name] shows an incorrect state.

summary: MCP tools bypass ToolRegistry — registered ad-hoc in SessionTools.resolve()
detail: MCP tools are not registered in ToolRegistry. Instead, SessionTools.resolve() at session/tools.ts:159 calls mcp.tools() and directly adds entries to the tools Record. This means ToolRegistry-based invalidation (mcp.tools_changed → invalidate tool_registry_context at context/invalidation-registry.ts:56) may not clear MCP tool representations.

summary: MCP lifecycle test exists (888 lines, 20 tests) but major gaps remain: OAuth, tool execution untested
detail: Correction: there IS a dedicated test at test/mcp/lifecycle.test.ts. It covers: add, tools caching, tool change notifications, disconnect, reconnect, server replacement, partial failure, schema fallback, disabled servers, prompts, resources, NotFoundError, timeout cleanup, transport cleanup. Gaps: OAuth flow (startAuth/authenticate/finishAuth/removeAuth/hasStoredTokens) completely untested. Tool execution (callTool/getPrompt/readResource) untested. SSE-only behavior untested. Regression-proofs at test/campaign/regression-proofs.test.ts add 6 invariants.

summary: EffectBridge leak — new bridge created per storeClient but never disposed
detail: storeClient() creates a new EffectBridge.make() each time (line 639). When closeClient() removes the client, the bridge is not disposed. Over connect/disconnect cycles, unused bridges accumulate.

summary: HealthRegistry set only at MCP init — never updated during runtime
detail: HealthRegistry is set to Healthy at layer construction and never updated. Disconnected/broken servers do not affect health. Health becomes a static decoration rather than a live signal — misleading to any health-check consumer.
